content: I am getting married, and I want to cancel my health care plan

Okay, I can help with that. Congratulations on your upcoming marriage!

However, getting married doesn't typically qualify you to cancel your health insurance altogether. Usually, it allows you to add your spouse to your plan. Are you sure you want to cancel your health plan instead of adding your spouse?

If you still want to cancel, could you please confirm the date of your marriage?
if I can't cancel, why do you need my date of marriage?

You're right to ask! I need the date of your marriage to determine the effective date for adding your spouse to your health plan and other benefits. Reporting your marriage also opens a 31-day window for you to make changes to your benefits, such as adding your spouse to your medical, dental, and vision coverage.
I don't want to add him in the plan. I want to cancel my plan

I understand. To clarify, are you wanting to cancel your health insurance coverage entirely due to your marriage?

While getting married is a qualifying life event, it generally allows you to make changes to your existing coverage, like adding a spouse. Canceling your own coverage might only be possible if you have another source of health insurance available to you through your spouse's employer, for example, which would then qualify as "loss of other coverage".

Can you confirm if you will have other health insurance coverage available to you after you get married? And, if so, what is the date that your new coverage will begin?
Yes, I am going on my husband health crea plan

Okay, great. In that case, you can use your marriage as a qualifying event to enroll in your husband's health plan, and the loss of your current coverage here also qualifies you to cancel your current plan.

What is the date of your marriage and when will you be covered by your husband's plan?

Okay, thanks for confirming. To proceed, I need the exact date of your marriage, the date your new coverage starts, and the specific date you would like to cancel your current health insurance coverage. Note that, generally, your current coverage can continue until the day before your new coverage begins.
Okay, so you're getting married on March 1st, and your new health insurance coverage also starts on March 1st?

Okay, great. I can report your marriage as a life event, and then cancel your health insurance coverage, effective March 1st.

Because you're canceling your health insurance, this also means you'll be cancelling your Healthcare FSA, since it is tied to your health insurance coverage. Is that okay?

To confirm:
*   Event Type: Marriage
*   Event Date: March 1, 2026
*   Cancel Health Insurance: Yes, effective March 1, 2026
*   Impact: Cancellation of Healthcare FSA

Do you wish to proceed?
Life event successfully reported. Your special enrollment window is now open until 31 days from the event date.
